essence: Improves machinery reliability by monitoring equipment health, preventing failures, and supporting long‑term maintenance strategies.

identity.title: Reliability Technician
identity.summary: Conducts inspections, collects diagnostic data, identifies failure risks, and supports reliability engineers in improving equipment performance and reducing downtime.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in advanced diagnostics, asset‑management systems, or high‑risk industrial environments can exceed £70k-£90k+.

qualifications: Training in mechanical engineering, reliability engineering, or maintenance operations
Knowledge of failure modes, asset management, and maintenance strategies
Ability to use diagnostic tools such as vibration analysers, thermal cameras, and oil‑analysis kits
Understanding of reliability‑centred maintenance (RCM) and predictive‑maintenance principles

lifestyle.description: Reliability technicians work for manufacturing plants, utilities, engineering firms, and industrial maintenance teams. They monitor equipment health, identify reliability risks, and support long‑term maintenance planning. The role suits individuals who enjoy diagnostics, engineering systems, and improving operational performance.

habits: Conducts equipment inspections and collects diagnostic data
Identifies early signs of wear, failure, or performance decline
Uses diagnostic tools and asset‑management software
